steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Derive a third equal pair from the ratio
steps.content: From $\frac{KL}{KJ}=\frac{ST}{SR}$ and $KL = ST = 28$, we get $\frac{28}{KJ}=\frac{28}{SR}$, so $KJ = SR$.

steps.title: Apply SAS to complete the proof
steps.content:
We now have three equalities:
  • $KJ = SR$
  • $\angle K = \angle S = 40^{\circ}$
  • $KL = ST = 28$
In each triangle, the angle is between its two sides. By Side-Angle-Side (SAS) congruence, $\triangle JKL \cong \triangle RST$ with the given information alone. D is correct.
